response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior software developer with a broad web and full-stack background, but the match to this embedded networking security role is weak. The position requires strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source-level analysis, native-level Japanese, and hands-on experience with L2/L3 switching and security technologies like MACsec and 802.1X — none of which are evidenced. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and his listed languages do not include Japanese, which is a hard requirement.
strengths: 18+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services) at Dunnhumby shows systems-level aptitude
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency (only English, Romanian, German listed) — a mandatory requirement
Minimal recent C programming and no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience
No demonstrated background in network switch development, L2/L3 protocols, or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal)

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded networking/security domain experience
Long tenure and systems programming aptitude
